headline: SMBC Aviation Capital Orders 100 Boeing 737 MAX Jets
summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] and SMBC Aviation Capital today announced that the global aviation finance platform and lessor has ordered 100 737 MAX airplanes, including 60 737-10 and 40 737-8 jets.

content.title: SMBC Aviation Capital Orders 100 Boeing 737 MAX Jets
content.summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] and SMBC Aviation Capital today announced that the global aviation finance platform and lessor has ordered 100 737 MAX airplanes, including 60 737-10 and 40 737-8 jets.

thumbnail.caption: Boeing and SMBC Aviation Capital today announced that the global aviation finance platform and lessor has ordered 100 737 MAX airplanes, including 60 737-10 and 40 737-8 jets.

headline: Riyadh Air Commits to Purchase 28 Boeing 787 Dreamliners, Adds 787-10 to Future Fleet
summary: Riyadh Air, the new national carrier of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and Boeing [NYSE: BA] today announced that the airline is exercising options for 28 more 787 Dreamliner jets as part of its growth plan. The agreement to exercise most of the options from Riyadh Air's 2023 order also includes the conversion of 20 airplanes to the larger 787-10 variant.

content.title: Riyadh Air Commits to Purchase 28 Boeing 787 Dreamliners, Adds 787-10 to Future Fleet
content.summary: Riyadh Air, the new national carrier of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and Boeing [NYSE: BA] today announced that the airline is exercising options for 28 more 787 Dreamliner jets as part of its growth plan. The agreement to exercise most of the options from Riyadh Air's 2023 order also includes the conversion of 20 airplanes to the larger 787-10 variant.

thumbnail.caption: Riyadh Air, the new national carrier of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and Boeing today announced that the airline is exercising options for 28 more 787 Dreamliner jets as part of its growth plan.

headline: Boeing CEO says new jet launch "a couple years away" amid rough balance sheet
summary: Investing.com -- Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg said Monday that the planemaker will need "a couple more years" to repair its finances before launching a new commercial jet. The company is focused on stabilizing its existing business rather than developing a successor to its best-selling 737 MAX.

content.title: Boeing CEO says new jet launch "a couple years away" amid rough balance sheet
content.summary: Investing.com -- Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg said Monday that the planemaker will need "a couple more years" to repair its finances before launching a new commercial jet. The company is focused on stabilizing its existing business rather than developing a successor to its best-selling 737 MAX.

headline: Boeing gets order for 100 737 MAX jets from leasing company SMBC
summary: US aircraft maker Boeing said Monday that it has received an order for 100 of its 737 MAX jets from leasing company SMBC Aviation Capital."Our partnership with Boeing goes back more than two decades, and this order reflects market momentum, as our airline and investor customers look to increase capacity with the 737-10," said SMBC chief executive Peter Barrett.

content.title: Boeing gets order for 100 737 MAX jets from leasing company SMBC
content.summary: US aircraft maker Boeing said Monday that it has received an order for 100 of its 737 MAX jets from leasing company SMBC Aviation Capital."Our partnership with Boeing goes back more than two decades, and this order reflects market momentum, as our airline and investor customers look to increase capacity with the 737-10," said SMBC chief executive Peter Barrett.

thumbnail.caption: Boeing aims to stage a major comeback at the Farnborough Air Show following production quality issues, lawsuits, and an overhaul of its governance (GREG BAKER)

content.summary: During an interview with CNBC, Ortberg said that Boeing is currently focusing on “repairing its finances” instead of rushing to develop a successor to the 737 Max.

headline: Boeing and Philippine Airlines Announce Commitment for up to 20 787 Dreamliner Jets
summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] and Philippine Airlines today announced the flag carrier has committed to order up to 20 787 Dreamliner jets. Once finalized, the agreement for 15 787-10 airplanes, with opportunity to purchase five more, will support Philippine Airlines' fleet modernization and expansion plans.

content.title: Boeing and Philippine Airlines Announce Commitment for up to 20 787 Dreamliner Jets
content.summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] and Philippine Airlines today announced the flag carrier has committed to order up to 20 787 Dreamliner jets. Once finalized, the agreement for 15 787-10 airplanes, with opportunity to purchase five more, will support Philippine Airlines' fleet modernization and expansion plans.

thumbnail.caption: Boeing and Philippine Airlines today announced at the Farnborough Airshow the flag carrier has committed to order up to 20 787 Dreamliner jets.

headline: FAA nears certification of Boeing 737 MAX 7 and MAX 10
summary: Boeing (NYSE:BA) could soon receive Federal Aviation Administration approval for its 737 MAX 7 and 737 MAX 10 aircraft, with a senior FAA official indicating certification of both variants is approaching following an extensive regulatory review. Speaking to Reuters at the Farnborough International Airshow, Deputy FAA Administrator Chris Rocheleau said certification of the 737 MAX 7 is “literally around the corner,” with approval for the larger MAX 10 expected shortly afterward.

content.title: FAA nears certification of Boeing 737 MAX 7 and MAX 10
content.summary: Boeing (NYSE:BA) could soon receive Federal Aviation Administration approval for its 737 MAX 7 and 737 MAX 10 aircraft, with a senior FAA official indicating certification of both variants is approaching following an extensive regulatory review. Speaking to Reuters at the Farnborough International Airshow, Deputy FAA Administrator Chris Rocheleau said certification of the 737 MAX 7 is “literally around the corner,” with approval for the larger MAX 10 expected shortly afterward.

headline: Leasing firm SMBC orders 200 planes from Airbus and Boeing
summary: Leasing firm SMBC Aviation Capital ordered 200 new medium-sized aircraft from Boeing and Airbus at the start of the landmark Farnborough Air Show on Monday, the companies said.US plane maker Boeing said it received an SMBC order for 100 of its 737 MAX jets and its European rival Airbus said the firm ordered another 100 of its popular A320neo.

headline: Ryanair CFO: Rival Airlines Could Fail This Winter; Industry Shakeout Could Lead to Pricing Power
summary: Ryanair's CFO just warned that several European airlines may not survive the coming winter, and he thinks that spells opportunity rather than crisis for his carrier. The reasoning behind that confidence reveals a cost structure gap so wide it barely seems like the same industry.
